scope: Authored viewport surface reconstruction only. The shared PRINT VIZ JUN2024 group stores color and assigns a filament shader; it does not slice the mesh or create a G-code toolpath.

looseGuide.browserBehavior: Retained for interactive inspection and hidden only from the authored surface capture because Blender does not rasterize the unbeveled curve.

geometry.role: The fixed demo fillets and fills the supplied outline, extrudes the surface, and passes the resulting mesh through the material-preview group.

capture.lightScaleInterpretation: Blender Area energy and Three.js RectAreaLight intensity use renderer-specific units. A 5/10/20/24/25/26/28/40/80 browser sweep selected 25 for near-zero mean-luminance error while preserving the best large-scale appearance.

recoveredGraph.frontColor: The filament material reads col and mixes it through its two HSV branches.
recoveredGraph.backColor: Geometry Backfacing selects the secondary Principled branch.

interpretation: Authored mode now renders the actual red col field and the shared Generated-coordinate filament Wave/Bump graph on the exact filleted plate. Surface-only framing removes the loose guide from the renderer comparison without removing it from the interactive browser asset. The silhouettes align at 0.9967 IoU and mean exposure differs by only -0.0074 luminance. The remaining fine layer-band and hue differences are Eevee/Three.js lighting, derivative, and tone-mapping residuals. This verifies the Blender viewport preview, not slicing or G-code.
